data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "Harness strictly beats self-consistency when the candidate error rate e satisfies k/(k+2) > e > 1/(k+1) for odd k≥7 AND the critic achieves precision P > e and recall R > 1-e on the subset of pools where the majority margin is ≤2 votes. Under these conditions, the critic removes more incorrect-fragile majorities than correct-fragile majorities, yielding net positive benefit.", "mechanism": "Let M = floor(k/2)+1 be the SC majority threshold. At error rate e, the probability of a fragile majority (margin ≤2) is maximized near e ≈ (k-M+1)/k = 2/k for k=7. Within this band, both fragile-correct and fragile-incorrect pools exist. The critic acts as a binary classifier on these pools. The expected net benefit per question = R·P_inc·N_inc - FPR·P_cor·N_cor, where P_inc = fraction of fragile pools that are incorrect, P_cor = fraction that are correct. Near e = 2/k for k=7, P_inc ≈ 0.29, P_cor ≈ 0.71. For net benefit > 0: R·0.29 > (1-P)·0.71, i.e., R/P > 2.45/(R+...). Simplifying: if R > 1-e and P > e, the critic's positive likelihood ratio exceeds the class prior ratio, guaranteeing net benefit on the fragile subset.", "scope": {"covers": ["odd k ≥ 7", "e ∈ (1/(k+1), k/(k+2))", "P > e AND R > 1-e on fragile-majority subset", "binary-answer questions only"], "excludes": ["k ≤ 5", "e < 1/(k+1) (SC ceiling)", "e > k/(k+2) (SC floor)", "P ≤ e (critic no better than chance)", "multi-answer questions where plurality ≠ majority"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "At k=7, e≈0.25, P>0.25, R>0.75: harness - SC ≤ 0 across all tested sub-bands", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "Net fixed-minus-broken ≤ 0 when P > e and R > 1-e on the fragile-majority subset", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "Harness improvement is uncorrelated with the fraction of fragile-majority pools in the sample (Spearman ρ < 0.3, p > 0.05)", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}], "prose_notes": "This candidate formalizes the threshold conditions more precisely than the first: P > e and R > 1-e are necessary AND sufficient because they guarantee the critic's likelihood ratio exceeds the prior odds on the fragile subset. The experiment should use a difficulty-targeted question set (not random sampling) to ensure enough questions fall in the e ≈ 2/k band. With 200 questions at k=7 (1400 candidate calls + 200 critic calls + 200 SC calls ≈ 1800 calls), this fits within 300k tokens if questions are concise."}
